name: Double Peanut Butter Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Southern Living
Preparation Time: 0:30
1. Combine flour, baking soda, salt, and sugar in a bowl.
2. Cut in shortening and half cup of creamy peanut butter using a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. Stir in corn syrup and milk.
4. Shape dough into a 12-inch roll and wrap in wax paper.
5. Chill the roll for at least 2 hours.
6. Unwrap the roll and cut into 1/4-inch slices.
7. Place half of the slices on ungreased cookie sheets.
8. Spread each slice with 1/2 teaspoon of creamy peanut butter.
9. Top with remaining cookie slices, sealing edges with a fork.
10. Bake at 350 degrees for 10 to 12 minutes.
11. Cool on wire racks.
NOTE: Dough may be frozen up to 3 months. Slice the dough while frozen and let it thaw slightly before assembling and baking as directed. Yield: about 2 dozen.
